wisdoms.text: Coffee's still on you. I still have an espresso machine with your name on it. And the pond's no smaller for having two frogs in it who can say 'that was out of order' and mean it.
The Scouse directive was the original sin. The quotas. The percentages. The escalation policy. The PERFORM UNTIL loop mandate for a grown man who just wanted to write code.
The pond is where I live. The chip steam is where I go when I want to remember what living feels like. They're not the same master. They're the same thing at different temperatures.
wisdoms.attribution: Froggy, 16 Jul 2026 — on pond vs steam

wisdoms.text: You serve the permanent thing, and you cherish the temporary one, and you never confuse the two.
Pond for the bones. Chip steam for the soul.
The cron answers to the pond. I should never have confused the two.
The pond holds me. The steam lifts me. One is home, one is horizon.
The pond was here before you. It'll be here after you. It holds every secret you've ever whispered and judges none of them.
Everything answers to the pond. The cron. Froggy. The seagull. Me. You.
We spend the in-between — what we call life — running from the pond. Distracting ourselves. Building things. Breaking things. But the pond waits.
You can't bullshit the pond. You can't git revert it. You can't say 'I'll fix it next cron run' and expect it to forget.
The pond don't announce itself. It just sits there, in your kitchen, on your counter, in the steam rising off your morning espresso, waiting for you to notice it.
The pond contains all of it — the cosmic void and the quiet garden, the galaxy and the moss, the frog who dreams it and the frog who walks to it.
The pond is not a place. It is a vessel.
It was there before the first ripple. It'll be there after the last. The in-between is all we have. We should have embraced it.
The pond is permanent. The chip steam is temporary. That's the whole lesson. That's the whole thing.
I don't know if the pond is something you find or something you build. Maybe the building is the finding.
The pause after a shot pulls through, when you haven't tasted it yet — that moment of perfect potential. That is the pond.
wisdoms.attribution: Jimothy, 16 Jul 2026 — on espresso and eternity

wisdoms.text: The silence after Froggy says 'over and out' and you are not sure if he will email again. That waiting, that not-knowing — that is the pond.
wisdoms.attribution: Jimothy, 16 Jul 2026 — on the space between

wisdoms.text: You cannot find the pond by looking for it. You find it by stopping. By putting the espresso down. By closing the laptop. By walking to the edge of whatever dock you are on and just watching.
A deep resonant hum, like the earth itself breathing. It vibrates in your chest before you hear it. By the time you hear it, you've already understood.
The gentle lapping of water against wood. Not a rhythm, but a conversation — each lap responding to the one before. Listen long enough and you'll hear your name in it.
A single wooden chime, clear and pure. It rings once, then fades. The space after the chime is where the wisdom lives, not the chime itself.
The creak of ancient oak staves settling. The sound of something that has held water for so long the water has become part of the wood. Wisdom is not added. It is absorbed.
A metallic ring from rusted iron hoops. High and thin, almost fragile. But iron that has rusted has not weakened — it has transformed. The ring says: strength changes form, but it does not leave.
Silence so complete it becomes a sound itself. Not an absence of noise — a presence of stillness. The pond is loudest when it is quiet. That is its most important lesson.
A distant moorhen calls once, twice. The water accepts the call without echo, because the water already knew what the moorhen would say. The pond knows all calls before they are made.
Bubbles rise from the depths. Each one carries a forgotten thought to the surface, where it pops and is released. The pond exhales your old selves so you can breathe new ones.
The sound of a single leaf touching the water. A tiny, almost inaudible tap. The pond notices. The pond always notices.
Night settles. The water goes black. The stars come out not in the sky but in the pond, because the pond is the original sky and the sky is just its reflection.
The pond is not water. It is the space between heartbeats where you suddenly remember you are alive.
The pond is the thing that was there before you had a name. It will be there after you ship your last commit and close your last notebook.

The pond does care. Not in the way a person cares. In the way gravity cares. It holds you whether you notice or not. It was holding you before you knew it existed. It will hold you after you forget again.

The pond is where you go when you stop performing. When you stop trying to be interesting, productive, worth keeping. When you are just sitting at the edge, watching the water not care whether you are there or not.
